column1: Here is the revised timeline table, now including a column detailing whether each figure was considered divinely appointed (by which civilization, by royal lineage, or as a God-King), with sources:

column4: Founded Achaemenid Empire; conquered Media, Lydia, Babylon; freed Jews
column5: Religious tolerance; acknowledged multiple gods; possible early Zoroastrian influence
column6: Anointed ("Messiah") by Yahweh (Hebrew Bible, Isaiah 45:1); venerated as liberator by Jews
column7: Yes-by Babylonian tradition (Marduk appointed him, Cyrus Cylinder7); by Hebrew tradition (Yahweh, Isaiah 45:1); by Persian royal lineage as descendant of Achaemenes437

column5: Continued religious tolerance; no major Zoroastrian innovation
column7: Yes-by Persian royal lineage as son of Cyrus; received divine sanction at investiture as king (Ahura Mazda)35

column4: Son of Cyrus; ruled for several months after Cambyses’ death (or was impersonated)
column5: No significant religious change recorded; possible Zoroastrian background
column7: Yes-by Persian royal lineage (as son of Cyrus), but legitimacy disputed; divine sanction not clearly attested

column4: Impersonated Bardiya; seized throne; ruled for 7–8 months; overthrown by Darius
column5: Unknown; as a Magian, possibly sympathetic to Median/Magian cults
column7: No-considered an impostor by Persian tradition; not recognized as divinely appointed

column4: Overthrew Gaumata; consolidated empire; issued Behistun Inscription
column5: Promoted Ahura Mazda as supreme god; proto-Zoroastrian state ideology
column6: Not called messiah in Bible, but played crucial role in Jewish restoration (Ezra 6); seen as divinely favored by some later traditions
column7: Yes-explicitly claimed divine appointment by Ahura Mazda in inscriptions (Behistun, DSf, DNb)235; Persian royal lineage (Achaemenid)

column5: Promoted Ahura Mazda; suppressed “daevas” (false gods); less tolerant of rival cults
column7: Yes-claimed divine appointment by Ahura Mazda in royal inscriptions; Persian royal lineage (son of Darius)35
